title: Pogo Storm: Surge Battery
hypothesis: A self-recovering two-stomp battery plus an explicit 1-to-100 horde ramp with telegraphed surge waves will turn repeated Space presses into a readable timing decision while delivering the requested conspicuous escalation without changing Relay Overdrive's movement or powers.
changesFromParents: Adds a 100-point STOMP battery: valid airborne stomps cost 44, recovery begins after a 0.45-second delay at 24 points per second, and under-cost presses are blocked without changing downward velocity.

Replaces sparse pattern waves with an exact target curve of 1 + floor(clamp(elapsed / 30, 0, 1) * 99), fast refill toward target, a 125-foe hard cap, and warned surge waves every 6.8 seconds after the first 6.4-second warning.
preserve: automatic pogo launch and landing loop
direct Capacitor, Lancer, and Phase unlocks at 1.7, 5.7, and 9.7 seconds
Capacitor landing wave and echo, triple-target Lancer, and rechargeable Phase contact conversion
skitter, hover, and charger threat vocabulary
sparse grayscale geometry with one gold accent
one authoritative player with attached body, halo, spring, boot, guide, guard, and weapon visuals

provenance.report: Responds narrowly to the latest 4/5, 59.7-second feedback. STOMP starts full at 100. A valid airborne primary spend costs 44, pauses regeneration for 0.45 seconds, then regenerates at 24 per second; an under-cost press leaves velocity untouched, gives restrained EMPTY/RECOVER feedback, and emits throttled blocked telemetry. The compact bar has a visible cost marker and CHARGED, READY, SPENT, RECOVER, and EMPTY states. Horde target is exactly 1 at start, 34 at 10 seconds, 67 at 20, and 100 at 30, then holds. A refill budget of 8 + target times 0.20 foes per second restores kills toward target. Starting at 6.4 seconds, a 0.9-second warning precedes surges every 6.8 seconds; each adds min(22, 8 + floor(elapsed / 3)) foes above target under a 125 cap. Surge composition cycles the preserved skitter, hover, and charger patterns. Sparse ramp checkpoints, surge warning/release, energy spend, and throttled energy-block events expose the requested measurements. No pickups, followers, menus, assets, dependencies, or runtime integrations are added.
